CellAttributeWithPoint.name: CellAttributeWithPoint
CellAttributeWithPoint.brief: The concept CellAttributeWithPoint is a refinement of the CellAttribute concept, to represent a cell attribute containing a point.

members.name: Point
members.brief: Type of the used point.

members.name: Info
members.brief: Type of the information, defined in the CellAttribute concept.

members.name: CellAttributeWithPoint
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.name: CellAttributeWithPoint
members.brief: Constructor initializing the point of this attribute by the copy constructor Point (apoint) .

members.name: CellAttributeWithPoint
members.brief: Constructor initializing the point of this attribute by the copy constructor Point (apoint) and initializing the information of this attribute by the copy constructor Info (info) .
members.detailed: Defined only if Info is different from void .

members.name: point
members.brief: Returns the point of this attribute.

members.name: point
members.brief: Returns the point of this attribute, when this is const.

CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point.name: CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point
CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point.brief: The class Cell_attribute_with_point represents an attribute containing a point and containing an information when Info_ is different from void .
CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point.detailed: This class can typically be used to associate a point to each 0-cell of a combinatorial or a generalized map. Is model of CellAttributeWithPoint LCC a model of the LinearCellComplex concept. Info_ the type of the information contained in the attribute, void for no information. Equal to void by default. Tag is ::Tag_true to enable the storage of a Dart_descriptor of the associated cell, ::Tag_false otherwise. Equal to ::Tag_true by default. OnMerge a functor called when two attributes are merged. Equal to Null_functor by default. OnSplit a functor called when one attribute is split in two. Equal to Null_functor by default. CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items <d> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc>

CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point_and_id.name: CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point_and_id
CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point_and_id.brief: The class Cell_attribute_with_point_and_id represents an attribute containing a point, containing an information when Info_ is different from void , and having an id.
CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point_and_id.detailed: This class can typically be used to associate a point with id to each 0-cell of a combinatorial or a generalized map. Is model of CellAttributeWithPoint LCC a model of the LinearCellComplex concept. Info_ the type of the information contained in the attribute, void for no information. Equal to void by default. Tag is ::Tag_true to enable the storage of a Dart_descriptor of the associated cell, ::Tag_false otherwise. Equal to ::Tag_true by default. OnMerge a functor called when two attributes are merged. Equal to Null_functor by default. OnSplit a functor called when one attribute is split in two. Equal to Null_functor by default. CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc>

members.name: id
members.brief: Returns the index.

members.name: id
members.brief: Returns the index by reference.

CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinator.name: CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinator.brief: The class Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map represents a linear cell complex in dimension d , in an ambient space of dimension d2 , using a combinatorial map as underlying combinatorial data-structure.
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinator.detailed: Like for Combinatorial_map , two versions exist: one where Darts and non void attributes are stored in memory using Compact_container , using Alloc as allocator, and use handles as descriptors; a second one where Darts and non void attributes are stored in an internal std::vector like data-structure, and use indices as descriptors. The choice between the two versions is done through the item class. Is model of LinearCellComplex CombinatorialMap d the dimension of the combinatorial map. d2 the dimension of the ambient space. Equal to d by default. LCCTraits be a model of the LinearCellComplexTraits concept, satisfying LCCTraits::ambient_dimension ==d2 . Equal to CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits <d2> by default. Items a model of the LinearCellComplexItems concept. Equal to CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items by default. Alloc has to match the standard allocator requirements. Equal to CGAL_ALLOCATOR(int) by default. Advanced Note that there is an additional, and undocumented, template parameter CMap for Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc,CMap> allowing to inherit from any model of the CombinatorialMap concept. CGAL::Combinatorial_map<d,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits <d,K> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items <d>

members.name: ambient_dimension
members.brief: Ambient dimension, must be > 1.

CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalize.name: CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalize.brief: The class Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map represents a linear cell complex in dimension d , in an ambient space of dimension d2 , using a generalized map as underlying combinatorial data-structure.
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalize.detailed: Like for GeneralizedMap , two versions exist: one where Darts and non void attributes are stored in memory using Compact_container , using Alloc as allocator, and use handles as descriptors; a second one where Darts and non void attributes are stored in an internal std::vector like data-structure, and use indices as descriptors. The choice between the two versions is done through the item class. Is model of LinearCellComplex GeneralizedMap d the dimension of the generalized map. d2 the dimension of the ambient space. Equal to d by default. LCCTraits a model of the LinearCellComplexTraits concept, satisfying LCCTraits::ambient_dimension ==d2 . Equal to CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits <d2> by default. Items a model of the LinearCellComplexItems concept. Equal to CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items <d> by default. Alloc has to match the standard allocator requirements. Equal to CGAL_ALLOCATOR(int) by default. Advanced Note that there is an additional, and undocumented, template parameter GMap for Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc,GMap> allowing to inherit from any model of the GeneralizedMap concept. CGAL::Generalized_map<d,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits <d,K> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items <d>

members.name: ambient_dimension
members.brief: Ambient dimension, must be > 1.

CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_incremental_bu.name: CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_incremental_builder_3
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_incremental_bu.brief: The auxiliary class Linear_cell_complex_incremental_builder_3 supports the incremental construction of linear cell complexes.
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_incremental_bu.detailed: LCC must be a model of the concept LinearCellComplex

members.name: Linear_cell_complex_incremental_builder_3
members.brief: Constructor.

members.name: begin_surface
members.brief: starts a new surface.

members.name: add_vertex
members.brief: adds a new vertex for p and returns its handle.

members.name: begin_facet
members.brief: starts a new facet.

members.name: add_vertex_to_facet
members.brief: adds vertex i at the end of the current facet.

members.name: end_facet
members.brief: ends the construction of the facet and returns the first dart of this facet.

members.name: end_surface
members.brief: ends the construction of the surface and returns one dart of the created surface.

members.name: add_facet
members.brief: is a synonym for begin_facet() , a call to add_vertex_to_facet() for each value in the range [first,beyond) , and a call to end_facet() .

CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items.name: CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items.brief: The class Linear_cell_complex_min_items defines void as the information associated with darts, and the attributes used.
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items.detailed: In this class, 0-attributes are enabled and associated with Cell_attribute_with_point . Is model of LinearCellComplexItems Example The following example shows one implementation of the Linear_cell_complex_min_items class. struct Linear_cell_complex_min_items { template < class LCC> struct Dart_wrapper { typedef CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_point<LCC> Vertex_attrib; typedef std::tuple<Vertex_attrib> Attributes; }; }; CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc>

CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits.name: CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits.brief: This geometric traits concept is used in the Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map and Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map classes.
CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_traits.detailed: It can take as parameter any model of the concept Kernel (for example any CGAL kernel), and defines inner types and functors corresponding to the given dimension. Is model of LinearCellComplexTraits d the dimension of the kernel, K a model of the concept Kernel if d==2 or d==3 ; a model of the concept Kernel_d otherwise. Equal by default to CGAL::Exact_predicates_inexact_constructions_kernel if d is 2 or 3, and is CGAL::Cartesian_d<double> otherwise. Note that the default argument used for K when d > 3 does not use exact predicates because operations that use predicates are only defined in 2D and 3D. CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map <d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc>

LinearCellComplex.name: LinearCellComplex
LinearCellComplex.brief: The concept LinearCellComplex represents a linear cell complex in dimension d , in an ambient space of dimension d2 . This is a model of the concept of GenericMap adding a requirement to ensure that each vertex of the map is associated with a model of CellAttributeWithPoint .
LinearCellComplex.detailed: Refines GenericMap Has models CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map<d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map<d,d2,LCCTraits,Items,Alloc> LinearCellComplexItems LinearCellComplexTraits

members.name: Vertex_attribute
members.brief: Type of 0-attributes, a model of CellAttributeWithPoint concept (a shortcut for Attribute_type<0>::type ).

members.name: Vertex_attribute_descriptor
members.brief: Descriptor through 0-attributes (a shortcut for Attribute_descriptor<0>::type ).

members.name: Vertex_attribute_const_descriptor
members.brief: Const descriptor through 0-attributes (a shortcut for Attribute_const_descriptor<0>::type ).

members.name: Vertex_attribute_range
members.brief: Range of all the 0-attributes, a model of the Range concept (a shortcut for Attribute_range<0>::type ).
members.detailed: Iterator inner type is bidirectional iterator and value type is Vertex_attribute .

members.name: Vertex_attribute_const_range
members.brief: Const range of all the 0-attributes, a model of the ConstRange concept a shortcut for Attribute_const_range<0>::type ).
members.detailed: Iterator inner type is bidirectional iterator and value type is Vertex_attribute .

members.name: ambient_dimension
members.brief: Ambient dimension, must be > 1.

members.name: LinearCellComplex
members.brief: Default constructor creating an empty linear cell complex.

members.name: vertex_attributes
members.brief: Returns a range of all the 0-attributes in this linear cell complex (a shortcut for attributes<0>() ).

members.name: vertex_attributes
members.brief: Returns a const range of all the 0-attributes in this linear cell complex (a shortcut for attributes<0>() const ).

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Returns true iff this linear cell complex is valid.
members.detailed: A linear cell complex lcc is valid if it is a valid generic map (cf. GenericMap::is_valid() ), and if for each dart descriptor d such that d $ \in $ darts() : vertex_attribute(d) != null_descriptor .

members.name: number_of_vertex_attributes
members.brief: Returns the number of 0-attributes in this linear cell complex (a shortcut for number_of_attributes<0>() ).

members.name: vertex_attribute
members.brief: Returns the 0-attribute associated with d .

members.name: vertex_attribute
members.brief: Returns the 0-attribute associated with d , when d is const.

members.name: point_of_vertex_attribute
members.brief: Returns the point in the 0-attribute v .

members.name: point_of_vertex_attribute
members.brief: Returns the point in the 0-attribute v , when v is const.

members.name: point
members.brief: Returns the point in the 0-attribute associated with d .

members.name: point
members.brief: Returns the point in the 0-attribute associated with d , when d is const.

members.name: create_dart
members.brief: Creates a new dart in this linear cell complex, sets its associated 0-attribute to v and returns the corresponding descriptor.

members.name: create_dart
members.brief: Creates a new dart in this linear cell complex, creates a new 0-attribute initialized with apoint , sets the associated 0-attribute of the new dart to this new 0-attribute, and returns the corresponding descriptor.

members.name: create_vertex_attribute
members.brief: Creates a new 0-attribute in this linear cell complex, and returns the corresponding descriptor (a shortcut for create_attribute<0>(t1) ).
members.detailed: Calls the constructor of Vertex_attribute having T1 as parameter. Overloads of this member function are defined that take from zero to nine arguments. With zero argument, create_vertex_attribute() creates a new 0-attribute by using the default constructor.

members.name: erase_vertex_attribute
members.brief: Removes the 0-attribute pointed to by v from this linear cell complex (a shortcut for erase_attribute<0>(v) ).

members.name: set_vertex_attribute
members.brief: Associates the 0-attribute of all the darts of the 0-cell containing d to v (a shortcut for set_attribute<0>(d,v) ).

members.name: correct_invalid_attributes
members.brief: Correct the invalid attributes of the linear cell complex.
members.detailed: We can have invalid attribute either if we have called set_automatic_attributes_management(false) before to use some modification operations or if we have modified the combinatorial map by using low level operations. The validation process of a linear cell complex validates its generic map (cf. correct_invalid_attributes() ), and for each dart d having no vertex attribute, a new vertex attribute is created, with its Point initialized to CGAL::Origin , and all the darts of the 0-cell containing d are linked with the new attribute.

members.name: barycenter
members.brief: Returns the barycenter of the i -cell containing d .

members.name: insert_point_in_cell
members.brief: Inserts a point, copy of p , in the i -cell containing d .
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on one dart of this cell. i $ \leq $ dimension $ \leq $ 2 and d $ \in $ darts() . If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if i -attributes are non void, Attribute_type<i>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called, with a the original i -attribute associated with d and a' each new i -attribute created during the operation. Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the combinatorial map can be no more valid after this operation.

members.name: insert_barycenter_in_cell
members.brief: Inserts a point in the barycenter of the i -cell containing d .
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on one dart of this cell. i $ \leq $ dimension $ \leq $ 2 and d $ \in $ darts() . If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if i -attributes are non void, Attribute_type<i>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called, with a the original i -attribute associated with d and a' each new i -attribute created during the operation. Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the combinatorial map can be no more valid after this operation.

members.name: insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2
members.brief: Inserts a 1-cell in the 2-cell containing d , the 1-cell being attached only by one of its vertex to the 0-cell containing d .
members.detailed: The second vertex is associated with a new 0-attribute containing a copy of p as point. Returns a descriptor on one dart belonging to the new 0-cell. 2 $ \leq $ dimension and d $ \in $ darts() . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the combinatorial map can be no more valid after this operation.

members.name: make_segment
members.brief: Creates an isolated segment in this linear cell complex (two darts linked by $ \beta_2$ ) having p0 , p1 as points.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on the dart associated with p0 . dimension $ \geq$ 2. Example of r=lcc.make_segment(p0,p1), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps. Example of r=lcc.make_segment(p0,p1), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps.

members.name: make_triangle
members.brief: Creates an isolated triangle in this linear cell complex having p0 , p1 , p2 as points.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on the dart associated with p0 and with edge [ p0 , p1 ]. dimension $ \geq$ 1. Example of r=lcc.make_triangle(p0,p1,p2), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps. Example of r=lcc.make_triangle(p0,p1,p2), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps.

members.name: make_quadrangle
members.brief: Creates an isolated quadrangle in this linear cell complex having p0 , p1 , p2 , p3 as points.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on the dart associated with p0 and with edge [ p0 , p1 ]. dimension $ \geq$ 1. Example of r=lcc.make_quadrangle(p0,p1,p2,p3), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps. Example of r=lcc.make_quadrangle(p0,p1,p2,p3), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps.
members.argsstring: (const Point &p0, const Point &p1, const Point &p2, const Point &p3)

members.name: make_tetrahedron
members.brief: Creates an isolated tetrahedron in this linear cell complex having p0 , p1 , p2 , p3 as points.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on the dart associated with p0 , with edge [ p0 , p1 ] and belonging to the 2-cell having p0 , p1 , p2 as points. dimension $ \geq$ 2. Example of r=lcc.make_tetrahedron(p0,p1,p2,p3), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps. Example of r=lcc.make_tetrahedron(p0,p1,p2,p3), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps.
members.argsstring: (const Point &p0, const Point &p1, const Point &p2, const Point &p3)

members.name: make_hexahedron
members.brief: Creates an isolated hexahedron in this linear cell complex having p0 , p1 , p2 , p3 , p4 , p5 , p6 , p7 as points.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on the dart associated with p0 , with edge [ p0 , p5 ] and belonging to the 2-cell having p0 , p5 , p6 , p1 as points. dimension $ \geq $ 2. Example of r=lcc.make_hexahedron(p0,p1,p2,p3,p4,p5,p6,p7), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps. Example of r=lcc.make_hexahedron(p0,p1,p2,p3,p4,p5,p6,p7), left for combinatorial map as combinatorial data-structure, right for generalized maps.
members.argsstring: (const Point &p0, const Point &p1, const Point &p2, const Point &p3, const Point &p4, const Point &p5, const Point &p6, const Point &p7)

LinearCellComplexItems.name: LinearCellComplexItems
LinearCellComplexItems.brief: The concept LinearCellComplexItems refines the concept of GenericMapItems by adding the requirement that 0-attributes are enabled, and associated with attributes that are models of the CellAttributeWithPoint concept.
LinearCellComplexItems.detailed: Refines GenericMapItems The first type in Attributes tuple must be a model of the CellAttributeWithPoint concept. Has models CGAL::Linear_cell_complex_min_items LinearCellComplex CellAttributeWithPoint

LinearCellComplexTraits.name: LinearCellComplexTraits
LinearCellComplexTraits.brief: Required types and functors for the LinearCellComplexTraits concept. This geometric traits concept is used in the Linear_cell_complex_for_combinatorial_map and Linear_cell_complex_for_generalized_map classes.

members.name: FT
members.brief: a number type that is a model of FieldNumberType.

members.name: Point
members.brief: point type.

members.name: Vector
members.brief: vector type.

members.name: Construct_translated_point
members.brief: Functor that provides Point operator() (const Point & p, const Vector & v) , which constructs the translation of point p by vector v , and Point operator() (const CGAL::Origin&, const Vector & v) , which constructs the translation of a point at the origin by vector v (used in barycenter() ).

members.name: Construct_vector
members.brief: Functor that provides Vector operator() (const Point & p1, const Point & p2) which constructs a vector as the difference of points p2-p1 , and Vector operator() (const CGAL::Origin&, const Point & p) which constructs a vector as the difference of point p and a point at the origin (used in barycenter and CGAL::read_plane_graph_in_lcc ).

members.name: Construct_sum_of_vectors
members.brief: Functor that provides Vector operator() (const Vector & v1, const Vector & v2) which constructs a vector as the sum of vectors v1+v2 (used in barycenter , CGAL::compute_normal_of_cell_0 and CGAL::compute_normal_of_cell_2 ).

members.name: Construct_scaled_vector
members.brief: Functor that provides Vector operator() (const Vector & v, FT scale) which constructs a vector equal to vector v scaled by scale factor (used in barycenter , CGAL::compute_normal_of_cell_0 and CGAL::compute_normal_of_cell_2 ).

members.name: Construct_midpoint
members.brief: Functor that provides Point operator() (const Point & p1, const Point & p2) which constructs the midpoint of points p1 and p2 (used in barycenter ).

members.name: Direction_2
members.brief: a model of Direction_2 .

members.name: Construct_direction_2
members.brief: a model of ConstructDirection_2 (used in CGAL::read_plane_graph_in_lcc ).

members.name: Construct_normal_3
members.brief: a model of ConstructNormal_3 (used in CGAL::compute_normal_of_cell_2 ).

members.name: Collinear_3
members.brief: a model of Collinear_3 (used in CGAL::compute_normal_of_cell_2 ).

members.name: ambient_dimension
members.brief: The ambient dimension (must be > 1).
